----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **What English Re-Centering would NOT change:** grammar (already centroid-aligned), French- and Latin-derived vocabulary (those words come from a different stratum), the \"th\" sounds (English and Icelandic kept Proto-Germanic þ/ð as dental fricatives; the other branches shifted them to stops: þ→d/t, ð→d, giving the systematic correspondence English *three/that/thin/thank/father* = German *drei/das/dünn/danken/Vater* = Dutch *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Swedish *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader*. This is a regular Grimm\'s Law correspondence, not drift, and not a target for Re-Centering), and words where the vowel shift moved all branches equally (no reform can help where every language went its own way). The reform is strict about its limits: it addresses spelling opacity, not deep phonological divergence, which happened to all branches.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | **What English Re-Centering would NOT change:** Grammar (already centroid-aligned), French- and Latin-derived Vocabulary (those Words come from a different Stratum), the \"th\" Sounds (English and Icelandic kept Proto-Germanic þ/ð as dental Fricatives; the other Branches shifted them to Stops: þ→d/t, ð→d, givin the systematic Correspondence English *three/that/thin/thank/father* = German *drei/das/dünn/danken/Vater* = Dutch *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Swedish *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader*. This is a regular Grimm\'s Law Correspondence, not Drift, and not a Target for Re-Centering), and Words where the Vowel Shift moved all Branches equally (no Reform can help where every Language went its own Way). The Reform is strict about its Limits: it addresses Spelling Opacity, not deep phonological Divergence, which happened to all Branches.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | **Wat Engelse Her-centrering NIET zou veranderen:** Grammatica (al op het Zwaartepunt afgestemd), Frans- en Latyns-afgeleid Vocabulaire (die Woorden komen uit een ander Stratum), de \"th\" Klanken (het Engels en Yslands behielden Proto-Germaans þ/ð als dentale Fricatieven; de andere Takken verschoven ze naar Plosieven: þ→d/t, ð→d, wat de systematische Correspondentie geeft: Engels *three/that/thin/thank/father* = Duits *drei/das/dünn/danken/Vater* = Nederlands *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Zweeds *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader*. Dit is een reguliere Grimm\'s Wet Correspondentie, geen Drift, en geen Doel voor Her-centrering), en Woorden waar de Klinker verschuiving alle Takken gelyk bewoog (geen Hervorming kan helpen waar elke Taal zyne eigen Weg ging). De Hervorming is strikt in zyne Beperkingen: het adresseert Spellings ondoorzichtigheid, niet diepe fonologische Divergentie, dat alle Takken overkwam.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | **Hvad Engelsk Recentrering IKKE ville forandre:** Grammatik (allerede Centroid-justeret), fransk- og latinskafledt Ordforraad (de Ord kommer fra et andet Lag), \"th\"-Lydene (Engelsk og Islandsk bevarede Protogermansk þ/ð som dentale Frikativer; de andre Grene forskoed dem til Lukkelyde: þ→d/t, ð→d, givende den systematiske Korrespondance Engelsk *three/that/thin/thank/father* = Tysk *drei/das/dünn/danken/Vater* = Nederlandsk *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Svensk *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader*. Dette er en regulaer Grimms Lovs Korrespondance, ikke Drift, og ikke et Maal for Recentrering), og Ord hvor Vokal Forskydningen bevaegede alle Grene lige meget (ingen Reform kan hjaelpe hvor hvert Sprog gik sin egen Vej). Reformen er streng om sine Graenser: den adresserer Stave Uigennemsigtighed, ikke dyb fonologisk Divergens, som skete for alle Grene.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | **Was Englisch Re-Centrierung NICHT aendern wuerde:** Grammatik (schon centroid-aligned), Franoesisch- und Lateinisch-derived Vokabular (diese Words kommen von einem verschiedenen Stratum), die \"th\" Sounds (Englisch und Islaendisch haben Proto-Germanisch þ/ð als dental Frikative kept; die anderen Branches haben sie tsu Stops geshifted: þ→d/t, ð→d, giving die systematische Korrespondents Englisch *three/that/thin/thank/father* = Deutsch *dry/das/duenn/danken/Vater* = Niederlaendisch *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Schwedisch *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader*. Das ist eine regulaere Grimm\'s Law Korrespondents, nicht Drift, und nicht ein Target fuer Re-Centrierung), und Words, wo der Vokal Shift hat alle Branches gleich bewegt (kine Reform kann helfen, wo jede Sprache ist ihren oigenen Weg gegangen). Die Reform ist strikt ueber ihre Limits: sie addressiert Spelling Opatsitaet, nicht tiefen phonologischen Divergents, was ist tsu allen Branches passiert.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | **Was das Englische Re-Centering NICHT ändern würde:** Grammatik (bereits Zentroid-konform), französisch- und lateinischstämmiges Vokabular (diese Wörter stammen aus einer anderen Schicht), die „th\"-Laute (Englisch und Isländisch bewahrten urgermanisches þ/ð als dentale Frikative; die anderen Zweige verschoben sie zu Plosiven: þ→d/t, ð→d, was die systematische Entsprechung Englisch *three/that/thin/thank/father* = Deutsch *drei/das/dünn/danken/Vater* = Niederländisch *drie/dat/dun/danken/vader* = Schwedisch *tre/det/tunn/tacka/fader* ergibt. Dies ist eine reguläre Grimm\'sche Entsprechung, keine Drift, und kein Re-Centering-Ziel) und Wörter, bei denen die Vokalverschiebung alle Zweige gleichermaßen betraf (keine Reform kann helfen, wo jede Sprache ihren eigenen Weg ging). Die Reform ist streng in ihren Grenzen: Sie behandelt Schreibungsopazität, nicht tiefe phonologische Divergenz, die in allen Zweigen stattfand.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Note:** This is explicitly speculative. No one is proposing English spelling reform; that conversation has a long and failed history. The point is to show that English, like German, has drift that is *historically contingent and reversible in principle*, even if impractical in fact. Understanding where each language drifted, and why, is what makes the centroid project intellectually coherent rather than arbitrary.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | **Note:** This is explicitly speculative. No one is proposing English Spelling Reform; that Conversation has a loni and failed History. The Point is to show that English, like German, has Drift that is *historically contingent and reversible in Principle*, even if impractical in Fact. Understanding where each Language drifted, and why, is what makes the Centroid Project intellectually coherent rather than arbitrary.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | **Opmerking:** Dit is expliciet speculatief. Niemand stelt Engelse Spellings hervorming voor; dat Gesprek heeft een lange en mislukte Geschiedenis. Het Punt is om te tonen dat het Engels, net als het Duits, Drift heeft dat *historisch contingent en in Principe omkeerbaar* is, zelfs als het in de Praktyk onpraktisch is. Begrypen waar elke Taal is afgedreven, en waarom, is wat het Zwaartepunt project intellectueel coherent maakt in plaats van willekeurig.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | **Note:** Dette er eksplicit spekulativt. Ingen forelaegger engelsk Stavereform; den Samtale har en lang og mislykket Historie. Pointen er at vise at Engelsk, som Tysk, har Drift der er *historisk betinget og i Princippet reversibel*, selv om upraktisk i Praksis. At forstaa hvor hvert Sprog driftede, og hvorfor, er hvad der goer Centroid Projektet intellektuelt sammenhaengende snarere end vilkaarligt.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | **Note:** Das ist explitsit spekulativ. Niemand proponiert englische Spelling Reform; diese Konversation hat eine lange und failed Historie. Der Point ist tsu tsoigen, dass Englisch, wie Deutsch, Drift hat, was ist *historisch kontingent und reversibel in Printsip*, even wenn unpraktisch in Fakt. Das Verstehen, wo jede Sprache ist drifted, und warum, ist das, was macht das centroid Projekt intellektuell koharent rather than arbitraer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | **Hinweis:** Dies ist ausdrücklich spekulativ. Niemand schlägt eine englische Rechtschreibreform vor; diese Diskussion hat eine lange Geschichte des Scheiterns. Der Punkt ist zu zeigen, dass Englisch, wie Deutsch, eine Drift aufweist, die *historisch kontingent und prinzipiell umkehrbar* ist, auch wenn sie in der Praxis unpraktikabel ist. Zu verstehen, wo jede Sprache abgedriftet ist und warum, macht das Zentroid-Projekt intellektuell kohärent statt willkürlich.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Case remnants: Re-Centering English morphology.** The spelling changes above address orthography. English also retains morphological case, not in its nouns (which lost it) but intact in its pronouns. The English pronoun system has kept the Germanic case oppositions for a thousand years: nominative *I / he / she / we / they / who*; oblique (accusative and dative merged) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; genitive *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. This maps directly onto German *ich/mich/mir/mein*, *er/ihn/ihm/sein*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen*. English collapsed accusative and dative into one oblique form (the same merger happening informally in many German dialects today) but kept the nominative/oblique/genitive three-way split in every pronoun. The apostrophe-s genitive (*the king\'s crown*) is the old Germanic genitive marker, exactly equivalent to German *des Königs Krone* and Swedish *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                                               | **Case Remnants: Re-Centering English Morphology.** The Spelling Changes above address Orthography. English also retains morphological Case, not in its Nouns (which lost it) but intact in its Pronouns. The English Pronoun System has kept the Germanic Case Oppositions for a thousand Years: nominative *I / he / she / we / they / who*; oblique (accusative and dative merged) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; genitive *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. This maps directly onto German *ich/mich/mir/mein*, *er/ihn/ihm/sein*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen*. English collapsed accusative and dative into one oblique Form (the same Merger happening informally in many German Dialects today) but kept the nominative/oblique/genitive three-way Split in every Pronoun. The apostrophe-s genitive (*the king\'s crown*) is the old Germanic genitive Marker, exactly equivalent to German *des Königs Krone* and Swedish *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                                               | **Naamval resten: het Her-centreren van Engelse Morfologie.** De Spellings veranderingen hierboven adresseren Orthografie. Het Engels behoudt ook morfologische Naamval, niet in zyne Zelfstandige naamwoorden (dat die verloren) maar intact in zyne Voornaamwoorden. Het Engelse Voornaamwoord systeem heeft de Germaanse Naamvals opposities duizend Jaar behouden: Nominatief *I / he / she / we / they / who*; Obliquus (Accusatief en Datief versmolten) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; Genitief *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. Dit komt direct overeen met Duits *ich/mich/mir/mein*, *er/ihn/ihm/sein*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen*. Het Engels stortte Accusatief en Datief in tot een Obliquus vorm (dezelfde Versmelting dat informeel in veel Duitse Dialecten plaatsvindt vandaag de Dag) maar behield de Nominatief/Obliquus/Genitief driewegs Splitsing in elk Voornaamwoord. De Apostrof-s Genitief (*the king\'s crown*) is de oude Germaanse Genitief markeerder, exact equivalent aan Duits *des Königs Krone* en Zweeds *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | **Kasus Rester: Recentrering af Engelsk Morfologi.** Stave Forandringerne ovenfor adresserer Ortografi. Engelsk bevarer ogsaa morfologisk Kasus, ikke i sine Navneord (som mistede det) men intakt i sine Pronominer. Det engelske Pronomen System har bevaret de germanske Kasus Modsaaetninger i tusind Aar: Nominativ *I / he / she / we / they / who*; Oblik (Akkusativ og Dativ sammenslaaet) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; Genitiv *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. Dette kan direkte overfoeres til Tysk *ich/mich/mir/mein*, *er/ihn/ihm/sein*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen*. Engelsk kollapsede Akkusativ og Dativ til én Oblik Form (den samme Sammensmeltning der sker uformelt i mange tyske Dialekter i Dag) men bevarede det Tre Vejs Nominativ/Oblik/Genitiv Skel i hvert Pronomen. Apostrof-s Genitiven (*the king\'s crown*) er den gamle germanske Genitiv Markoer, praecis aekvivalent til Tysk *des Königs Krone* og Svensk *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                  | **Fall Remnants: Re-Centrierung englische Morphologie.** Die Spelling Aenderungen oben addressieren Orthographie. Englisch retainiert auch morphologischen Fall, nicht in sinen Nouns (wo haben ihn verloren), aber intakt in sinen Pronouns. Das englische Pronoun System hat die germanischen Fall Oppositionen fuer tausend Jahre kept: nominativ *I / he / she / we / they / who*; oblique (akkusativ und dativ merged) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; genitiv *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. Das mappt direkt onto Deutsch *ich/mich/mir/mine*, *er/ihn/ihm/sine*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen*. Englisch hat akkusativ und dativ collapsed into eine oblique Form (der selbe Merger, wo passiert informally in vielen Deutsch Dialekten hoite), aber hat den nominativ/oblique/genitiv dry-way Split in jedem Pronoun kept. Der Apostrophe-s Genitiv (*the king\'s crown*) ist der alte germanische Genitiv Marker, genai equivalent tsu Deutsch *von dem Koenig sine Krone* und Schwedisch *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                                                                                         | **Kasusreste: Re-Centering der englischen Morphologie.** Die obigen Schreibänderungen betreffen die Orthographie. Englisch bewahrt auch morphologischen Kasus, nicht in seinen Substantiven (die ihn verloren), aber intakt in seinen Pronomen. Das englische Pronominalsystem hat die germanischen Kasusoppositionen seit tausend Jahren bewahrt: Nominativ *I / he / she / we / they / who*; Obliquus (Akkusativ und Dativ verschmolzen) *me / him / her / us / them / whom*; Genitiv *my / his / her / our / their / whose*. Dies bildet direkt auf Deutsch *ich/mich/mir/mein*, *er/ihn/ihm/sein*, *wer/wen/wem/wessen* ab. Englisch verschmolz Akkusativ und Dativ zu einem Obliquus (derselbe Zusammenfall, der in vielen deutschen Dialekten heute informell stattfindet), bewahrte aber den Nominativ/Obliquus/Genitiv-Dreiwegkontrast in jedem Pronomen. Das Apostroph-s-Genitivum (*the king\'s crown*) ist der alte germanische Genitivmarker, exakt äquivalent zum deutschen *des Königs Krone* und schwedischen *kungens krona*.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
